local_dishes.name: Pad Thai Jay
local_dishes.description: Stir-fried rice noodles with tofu, peanuts, bean sprouts, and tamarind sauce (vegetarian version)

local_dishes.name: Green Curry with Tofu (Gaeng Keow Wan Jay)
local_dishes.description: Spicy Thai green curry with vegetables and tofu

local_dishes.name: Red Curry with Vegetables (Gaeng Daeng Jay)
local_dishes.description: Creamy coconut curry with seasonal vegetables and tofu

local_dishes.name: Massaman Curry with Tofu (Gaeng Massaman Jay)
local_dishes.description: Mild, slightly sweet curry with potatoes, peanuts, and tofu

local_dishes.name: Tom Yum Soup (Vegetarian Version)
local_dishes.description: Hot and sour soup with mushrooms, lemongrass, lime, and tofu

local_dishes.name: Som Tam Jay
local_dishes.description: Green papaya salad, often served without fish sauce for the vegetarian version

local_dishes.name: Spring Rolls (Poh Pia Jay)
local_dishes.description: Crispy vegetable-filled spring rolls

local_dishes.name: Stir-Fried Morning Glory (Pad Pak Boong Jay)
local_dishes.description: Spicy stir-fried water spinach with garlic and soy sauce

local_dishes.name: Mango Sticky Rice (Khao Niew Mamuang)
local_dishes.description: Sweet dessert of sticky rice with coconut milk and fresh mango

local_dishes.name: Fried Tofu with Peanut Sauce
local_dishes.description: Crispy tofu served with a rich peanut dipping sauce

local_dishes.name: Vegetable Fried Rice (Khao Pad Jay)
local_dishes.description: Thai-style fried rice with assorted vegetables and tofu

local_dishes.name: Thai Sweet Corn Cakes (Tod Man Khao Pod Jay)
local_dishes.description: Crispy corn fritters, usually vegetarian-friendly

local_dishes.name: Pad Thai (Vegetarian)
local_dishes.description: Thailand's most famous noodle dish made with rice noodles, tofu, peanuts, lime, and veggies

local_dishes.name: Som Tam (Papaya Salad – Veg Version)
local_dishes.description: A refreshing salad made with raw papaya, lime, chili, and peanuts

local_dishes.name: Thai Green Curry (Veg)
local_dishes.description: A fragrant coconut curry with Thai basil, eggplant, and vegetables

local_dishes.name: Tom Yum Soup (Vegetarian)
local_dishes.description: A hot and sour soup with lemongrass, kaffir lime, mushrooms, and herbs

local_dishes.name: Pad See Ew (Veg)
local_dishes.description: Flat rice noodles stir-fried with soy sauce, Chinese broccoli, and tofu

local_dishes.name: Mango Sticky Rice
local_dishes.description: Thailand's iconic dessert made with sweet sticky rice, coconut milk, and ripe mango

local_dishes.name: Morning Glory Stir-Fry (Pad Pak Boong)
local_dishes.description: Water spinach stir-fried with garlic and soy sauce. Simple, flavorful, and very local

local_dishes.name: Vegetarian Spring Rolls
local_dishes.description: Crispy rolls filled with glass noodles and vegetables. Served with sweet chili sauce

local_dishes.name: Massaman Curry (Veg)
local_dishes.description: A mild, slightly sweet curry with potatoes, peanuts, and coconut milk

local_dishes.name: Thai Fried Rice (Veg)
local_dishes.description: Jasmine rice stir-fried with vegetables, tofu, and sauces

local_dishes.name: Pineapple Fried Rice (Veg)
local_dishes.description: Rice cooked with pineapple, cashews, and veggies — often served in a pineapple shell

local_dishes.name: Thai Iced Tea (Veg-Friendly)
local_dishes.description: Sweet, creamy tea made with condensed milk

local_dishes.name: Pad Thai
local_dishes.description: Experience the exquisite blend of sweet, sour, and savoury notes that dance on your palate as skilled street vendors expertly toss wok-fried rice noodles with succulent shrimp, tender chicken, or crispy tofu
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Pattaya and Bangkok.pdf
Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Bangkok and Pattaya.pdf

local_dishes.name: Khanom Bueang (Crispy Crepes)
local_dishes.description: These delicate sweet or savory crepes come filled with various ingredients (choose vegetarian options or the sweet ones- coconut cream, or meringue. They offer a delightful textural contrast between the crisp exterior and the soft, flavorful filling
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Pattaya and Bangkok.pdf
Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Bangkok and Pattaya.pdf

local_dishes.name: Mango Sticky Rice with Coconut Cream (Khao Neow Mamuang)
local_dishes.description: This classic Thai dessert features perfectly ripe mango served with sticky rice cooked in coconut milk and often drizzled with a sweet coconut cream sauce. It's a simple yet satisfying way to end a meal
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Pattaya and Bangkok.pdf
Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Bangkok and Pattaya.pdf

local_dishes.name: Miang Kam
local_dishes.description: This bite-sized northern Thai snack features betel leaves filled with a flavorful mixture of chopped nuts, dried shrimp, ginger, and other ingredients. It's a fresh and healthy option with a complex flavor profile
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Pattaya and Bangkok.pdf
Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Bangkok and Pattaya.pdf

local_dishes.name: Som Tam
local_dishes.description: This spicy papaya salad is a dish made with shredded green papaya, fiery chillies, dried shrimp, tomatoes, crunchy peanuts and umami fish sauce
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Pattaya and Bangkok.pdf
Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Bangkok and Pattaya.pdf

local_dishes.name: Thai Green Curry
local_dishes.description: Thai Green Curry is a staple in Thailand- ask for the vegetarian version
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Pattaya and Bangkok.pdf

local_dishes.name: Cha Yen – Thai Tea
local_dishes.description: The vibrant orange hue is matched by the robust flavour of black tea infused with aromatic spices, vanilla, and orange blossom
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Pattaya and Bangkok.pdf

local_dishes.name: Coconut Ice Cream (I-dtim Mat Phrao)
local_dishes.description: Served in a coconut shell, this creamy delight is crowned with an array of toppings, from crunchy roasted peanuts to sweet corn and sticky rice
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Pattaya and Bangkok.pdf
Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Bangkok and Pattaya.pdf

local_dishes.name: Thai Green Curry Chicken (Gaeng Keow Wan Gai)
local_dishes.description: Tender chicken in fragrant green curry paste infused with herbs and spices
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Bangkok and Pattaya.pdf

local_dishes.name: Cha Yen (Thai Tea)
local_dishes.description: Vibrant orange tea with robust flavor of black tea infused with spices, vanilla, and orange blossom
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Bangkok and Pattaya.pdf

local_dishes.name: Khao Man Gai
local_dishes.description: Thai-style Hainanese chicken rice with spicy Ginger Sauce
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Bangkok and Pattaya.pdf

local_dishes.name: Pla Pao
local_dishes.description: Grilled fish dish embodying Thailand's coastal cuisine
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Bangkok and Pattaya.pdf

local_dishes.name: Thai Red Curry Noodle Soup
local_dishes.description: Creamy red curry broth with soft rice noodles, meat slices, and vegetables
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Bangkok and Pattaya.pdf

local_dishes.name: Tom Yum Goong
local_dishes.description: Spicy-sour shrimp soup with lemongrass, galangal, and lime
local_dishes.where_to_try: Pe Aor Tom Yum Kung Noodle, Siam area

local_dishes.name: Green Curry (Gaeng Keow Wan)
local_dishes.description: Spicy coconut curry with chicken or beef and Thai basil

local_dishes.name: Moo Ping
local_dishes.description: Charcoal-grilled pork skewers marinated in coconut milk and herbs
local_dishes.where_to_try: Chatuchak Market or Soi Rambuttri, Bangkok

local_dishes.name: Kai Yang (Grilled Chicken)
local_dishes.description: Smoky grilled chicken marinated with garlic, lemongrass, and coriander root
local_dishes.where_to_try: Sabai Jai Gai Yang, Bangkok (Ekkamai area)

local_dishes.name: Massaman Curry
local_dishes.description: Mild, fragrant curry with beef or chicken, peanuts, and potatoes

local_dishes.name: Khao Pad (Thai Fried Rice)
local_dishes.description: Fried rice with shrimp, crab, or chicken — flavored with fish sauce and lime
local_dishes.where_to_try: Any beachside restaurant in Phuket or Krabi

safety_tips.tip: Stay aware of your surroundings – Pickpocketing can happen in crowded areas and markets
Use reputable transport – Prefer metered taxis, Grab app, BTS/MRT, or licensed tuk-tuks
Keep valuables secure – Use a money belt or hidden pouch for cash, passport, and cards
Avoid unlicensed tours – Book excursions through trusted operators or your hotel
Be cautious with street food – Eat from busy stalls with high turnover for freshness
Avoid scams – Watch out for tuk-tuk scams, gem scams, and overly 'helpful' strangers
Stay hydrated and protected from the sun – Carry water, wear sunscreen and a hat
Follow local laws – Drug offenses and disrespecting the monarchy carry severe penalties
Keep emergency contacts handy – Local police, embassy, and hospital numbers
Avoid isolated areas at night – Stick to well-lit and populated streets
Respect the monarchy – Always show respect; avoid negative remarks about the royal family
Dress appropriately – Cover shoulders and knees when visiting temples
Use polite gestures – Wai (pressing palms together) is a common greeting
Don't touch someone's head – The head is considered sacred in Thai culture
Don't point your feet – Feet are considered the lowest part of the body; avoid pointing them at people or religious objects
Don't raise your voice – Loud arguments or confrontations are frowned upon

Stay alert in crowded areas – pickpocketing can occur in markets, night bazaars, buses, and tourist hotspots
Respect local laws & customs – e.g., no disrespect towards the Thai Royal Family (strict laws)
Use trusted transport – opt for Grab (ride-hailing app) or licensed taxis; avoid unmetered cabs
Secure your belongings – keep passport, cash, and valuables in a hotel safe or money belt
Drink responsibly – avoid accepting drinks from strangers in bars/clubs
Beach & water safety – obey warning flags, as currents can be strong during monsoon (August)
Stay hydrated – Thailand is hot & humid; carry water and wear sunscreen
Beware of Gem Scam – 'friendly' locals lure you to a gem shop claiming a great deal; gems are overpriced & low quality
Tuk-Tuk / Taxi Scams – drivers refusing meters or offering cheap all-day tours (usually end at commission shops)
Jet Ski Scam – rental operators falsely claim you damaged their jet ski; avoid or use reputable shops with photos taken before renting
Fake Tour Operators for day trips – overly cheap tours may be fake or cut corners; book through reliable agencies
Closed Attraction Scam – someone tells you a temple/attraction is 'closed' and redirects you to shops
ATM Fraud – use ATMs attached to banks; skimming devices may be used at standalone ATMs
Bar/Club Overcharging – always check the menu and bill before ordering
Pickpockets – especially in walking streets, floating markets, and festivals

connectivity_tips.tip: AIS (Advanced Info Service) – Widely used, good coverage across the city and tourist areas
DTAC (Total Access Communication) – Strong urban coverage, competitive tourist SIM packages
TrueMove H – Fast 4G/5G network, popular among travelers and locals
Where to buy SIM cards: Airports (Suvarnabhumi & Don Mueang), 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, mobile network stores
Documents required for SIM: Passport (mandatory for registration)
SIM types: Prepaid tourist SIMs with data, call, and text packages
Top-ups / Reloads: Available at convenience stores, network stores, or online
TrueMove H offers fast 4G/5G network, popular among travelers and locals and is top recommendation
AIS (Advanced Info Service) is widely used with good coverage across the city and tourist areas
DTAC (Total Access Communication) has strong urban coverage and competitive tourist SIM packages
Buy SIM cards at airports (Suvarnabhumi & Don Mueang), 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, or mobile network stores
Passport is mandatory for SIM card registration
Prepaid tourist SIMs available with data, call, and text packages
Top-ups available at convenience stores, network stores, or online
AIS (Advanced Info Service) – Widely used, good coverage across the city and tourist areas
DTAC (Total Access Communication) – Strong urban coverage, competitive tourist SIM packages
TrueMove H – Fast 4G/5G network, popular among travelers and locals
Buy SIM cards at airports (Suvarnabhumi & Don Mueang), 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, mobile network stores
Passport required for SIM card registration
Prepaid tourist SIMs available with data, call, and text packages
Top-ups available at convenience stores, network stores, or online
AIS, DTAC, and TrueMove H cover most tourist areas; rural areas may have weaker signal
AIS (Advanced Info Service) – Widely used, good coverage across the city and tourist areas.
DTAC (Total Access Communication) – Strong urban coverage, competitive tourist SIM packages.
TrueMove H – Fast 4G/5G network, popular among travelers and locals.
Buy SIM cards at airports (Suvarnabhumi & Don Mueang), 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, or mobile network stores.
Passport required for SIM card registration.
Prepaid tourist SIMs available with data, call, and text packages.
Top-ups available at convenience stores, network stores, or online.
You can purchase a True Move sim card. They have an amazing 5G network and offer tourists unlimited data. Packs start at Rs. 750 and you can pick up a sim at the airport.
connectivity_tips._source_file: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Pattaya and Bangkok.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: If you prefer, you can instead activate international roaming packs on your India number. Even if you choose not to purchase an international roaming pack, do ensure your roaming is activated to receive SMS such as for OTPs.

health_tips.tip: Routine vaccinations – Ensure you are up to date on MMR, DTP, polio, and influenza
Hepatitis A – Recommended for most travelers
Hepatitis B – Recommended if you might have medical procedures or close contact with locals
Typhoid – Recommended, especially if eating street food
Rabies – Consider if you'll have contact with animals or rural travel
Japanese Encephalitis – For long-term travelers or those visiting rural areas
Drink bottled or filtered water; avoid tap water
Eat cooked food and fruits you can peel; avoid raw salads from street vendors
Use insect repellent to prevent mosquito-borne diseases (dengue, chikungunya, Zika)
Stay hydrated and protect yourself from heat and sun exposure
Carry a basic first-aid kit and personal medications
Purchase travel insurance covering medical emergencies
Ensure you are up to date on routine vaccinations: MMR, DTP, polio, and influenza

Rabies – Consider if you'll have contact with animals or rural travel.
Japanese Encephalitis – For long-term travelers or those visiting rural areas.
Drink bottled or filtered water; avoid tap water.
Eat cooked food and fruits you can peel; avoid raw salads from street vendors.
Use insect repellent to prevent mosquito-borne diseases (dengue, chikungunya, Zika).
Stay hydrated and protect yourself from heat and sun exposure.
Carry a basic first-aid kit and personal medications.
Purchase travel insurance covering medical emergencies.
Wash hands frequently or use hand sanitizer.
Travel Insurance (we would recommend Tata AIG)

health_tips.tip: Doctors prescriptions/travel prescriptions for medicines (except over the counter meds)
health_tips._source_file: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Pattaya and Bangkok.pdf

health_tips.tip: No vaccines are mandatory for entry, but routine vaccinations and a few travel-related vaccines are recommended
Mosquito-borne illnesses include Dengue Fever (most common, especially in monsoon), Chikungunya, Malaria (rare in major cities, more in remote jungle/border areas), Japanese Encephalitis
Stick to bottled/filtered water (check seal). Avoid ice in drinks from street vendors
Eat freshly cooked food, avoid raw salads/seafood from unhygienic stalls
Stay hydrated (carry electrolyte packets). Wear sunscreen & a hat. Take breaks in shade/indoors
Carry prescriptions in original packaging + digital copy
Ensure routine vaccinations are up to date: MMR, DTP, polio, and influenza
Hepatitis A recommended for most travelers. Hepatitis B recommended if you might have medical procedures
Typhoid recommended, especially if eating street food. Consider rabies if contact with animals expected
Japanese Encephalitis for long-term travelers or those visiting rural areas
Drink bottled or filtered water; avoid tap water
Eat cooked food and fruits you can peel; avoid raw salads from street vendors
Use insect repellent to prevent mosquito-borne diseases (dengue, chikungunya, Zika)
Stay hydrated and protect yourself from heat and sun exposure
Carry a basic first-aid kit and personal medications. Purchase travel insurance covering medical emergencies
